# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI for External Contributions

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test
        continue-on-error: true
        id: test

      - name: Post test results as a comment
        if: always()
        run: |
          if [ "${{ steps.test.outcome }}" == "success" ]; then
            echo "All tests passed!" > result.txt
          else
            echo "Some tests failed." > result.txt
          fi
          COMMENT_BODY=$(cat result.txt)
          curl -X POST -H "Authorization: token ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" \
          -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
          https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/comments \
          -d "{\"body\": \"$COMMENT_BODY\"}"
